summary: Good‑quality audience recording of a 1994 show with a spectacular Playing in the Band > Drums > Wharf Rat jam and solid overall performance.
review: Both reviewers rate the tape four stars, noting that it sounds far better than most shed (audience) recordings and captures an especially inspired Playing‑in‑the‑Band > Drumz > Wharf Rat sequence. The show has highs like a funky Shakedown opener, a vivid Throwing Stones, and the beautiful Beatles cover The Rain, though the first set suffers from weaker moments such as Broken Arrow and Cheesy Answers.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed 4‑star reviews highlight a strong performance (especially the jam) and a good‑quality audience recording, yielding an overall rating just above four.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as far better than most shed tapes; clear enough to hear drums with ambient insect sounds, no major drop‑outs reported.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Drums > Wharf Rat
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong with a funky Shakedown but stalls in the middle; second set builds to an epic Playing‑in‑the‑Band jam that serves as the show’s peak before winding down into a powerful Wharf Rat.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His grizzled vocal on Wharf Rat is praised, and his guitar work on Throwing Stones is highlighted as a favorite.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums are noted as solid, with the Drumz segment adding excitement; ambient insect sounds add realism.
